beats.activity: Wake up, shower, breakfast with coffee and toast while checking the weather forecast.
beats.reasoning: Elena wakes at home in Queen Anne. Yesterday she had moderate energy (50/100) and was settling into a peaceful evening, so she's starting the day grounded but not rushed. The heat forecast makes her mentally prep for staying hydrated.

beats.activity: Light Rail commute from Queen Anne to South Lake Union library branch.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library, South Lake Union
beats.reasoning: Elena obsesses over transit schedules and yesterday's Light Rail was smooth; she'd naturally use it again on a hot day rather than walking or driving. 35 min is typical for Queen Anne to SLU via transit.

beats.activity: Morning shift at the library: shelving returns, staffing reference desk, helping patrons with research requests.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library, South Lake Union
beats.reasoning: Standard library workday. Elena is conscientious and high-achieving; she settles into the familiar rhythm of her job. The heat outside makes the air-conditioned library a comfortable anchor.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a salad and iced tea at a nearby café, eat indoors to escape the heat.
beats.reasoning: Elena favors the same café from yesterday (Solstice). With temps hitting 91F and air quality moderate from smoke, she stays indoors and hydrates. The familiar spot calms her anxious disposition.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift: cataloging, community programming prep for the zine workshop, helping patrons.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library, South Lake Union
beats.reasoning: Elena continues her work shift. She's excited about the zine workshop, so she invests energy into final preparations. The library remains her cool sanctuary as afternoon heat peaks.

beats.activity: Light Rail commute home to Queen Anne.
beats.reasoning: Elena uses Light Rail again on the way home. 35 min allows time for the short walk from station to her apartment, arriving by early evening.

beats.activity: Quick stop at Whole Foods to pick up fresh vegetables and a rotisserie chicken for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Elena is generous with money and values quality ingredients. A quick grocery run on her way home fits her routine and ensures she eats well. Still light enough to avoid the hottest part of the day lingering outdoors.

beats.activity: Prep and eat dinner at home: roasted vegetables with rotisserie chicken.
beats.reasoning: Elena settles at home to cook and eat dinner. She's already back in her neighborhood; eating at home is peaceful and fits her mood. Dinner prep gives her time to decompress from work.

beats.activity: Walk to Gas Works Park for the free outdoor concert; enjoy music and the cooling evening air.
beats.reasoning: The concert at Gas Works starts at 6pm; Elena arrives by 7:20pm to catch the tail end or the peak of the evening show. Gas Works is a 15-min walk from her Queen Anne home, and the cooling evening and park setting appeal to her. Though she prefers smaller gatherings, a public concert at a beloved landmark feels different—more about the place than the crowd. The air quality is moderate, and evening is cooler.

beats.activity: Walk home from Gas Works Park, enjoying the cool night air.
beats.reasoning: Elena heads home after the concert, 15 min walk back to Queen Anne. The evening is now cool and pleasant, and she's ready to wind down.

beats.activity: Wind down at home: read the foraging book, journal, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Elena settles into her evening routine at home, likely continuing the foraging book she was deep in yesterday. This quiet, solo wind-down aligns with her personality and sets up a smooth transition to sleep.
Elena sleeps 7h 45m, ending well before 6am the next day. A full night of rest after a productive, warm day.

diary: Woke up early enough to catch the sunrise from my kitchen window—coffee and toast felt right before the heat kicked in. Work was steady; the library was busy with people escaping the warmth, and I helped a researcher track down historical newspaper archives. Grabbed lunch at Solstice to stay cool, then spent the afternoon cataloging and prepping for next week's zine workshop. The whole day had this anticipatory buzz. By evening, I picked up groceries and threw together dinner, then walked over to Gas Works for the concert. The park was packed, but there was something nice about being surrounded by people all just... here together. The music drifted across the grass, and the smoke haze made the sunset look otherworldly. Walking home afterward felt like the perfect cool-down. Ended the night reading about mushroom foraging—felt grounded, somehow.
thought_process: The heat made me intentional about my choices today. I knew the afternoon would be brutal, so I built in time at the café and pushed my leisure time to evening when things cooled off. The wildfire smoke didn't bother me as much as I expected—if anything, it added this strange beauty to the light. I almost skipped the concert because of the crowds, but something told me to go anyway. I think I needed that sense of community, especially after a day of reference desk interactions. The mix of structure and spontaneity felt balanced—work gave me purpose, but the evening at Gas Works reminded me why I live here.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
